era: 1992-present. Grunge/Post. E=6 -- grunge context. Soul-inflected vocals over heavy rock. Capitol Hill.

theory_nodes.concept: Soul-grunge fusion: Shawn Smith's R&B/gospel-inflected voice over Stone Gossard's heavy rock architecture. Not a contrast or compromise -- a coherent aesthetic. The grunge tradition is wide enough to include Smith's vocal approach.
theory_nodes.description: Brad's defining aesthetic contribution: soul inflection in the grunge context.

theory_nodes.concept: Stone Gossard's Brad as Pearl Jam satellite: the same musicianship at intimate scale. Grunge architecture from inside the summit band, applied to a smaller creative project. The dual career model.
theory_nodes.description: Gossard's creative relationship between Pearl Jam and Brad.

theory_nodes.concept: E=6 as post-grunge calibration: heavy enough for the grunge tradition (Gossard's architecture), warm enough to remain Brad (Smith's voice). The soul inflection prevents E=7 even on the heaviest tracks.
theory_nodes.description: Why Brad's grunge context is E=6 and not E=7.

cross_references.relationship: E=6, first entry. Post-grunge context established. The 219-303-327 arc.
Second entry. Grunge catalog placement. Gossard-Pearl Jam connection documented.
Gossard's summit band. The grunge architecture that informs Brad's heaviest moments.
Gossard's proto-grunge origin. The riff vocabulary foundation.
E=6 holds. Both post-grunge Capitol Hill bands at the same energy calibration.
E=8 ahead. Sharp energy jump from post-grunge E=6 to heavy punk E=8.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=6 + E=7. Soul-grunge band vs. ultra-high-velocity summit wind. The intimate scale of Brad's grunge-context tracks against the exposure of the Rainier summit zone. E=13.
